notes: portal frame erection day on the warehouse. the steel crew from AusFab is here. 8 guys plus the dogman and crane operator
mobile crane is a 50 tonner, Liebherr LTM. massive thing. took about an hour to set up the outriggers and boom
first portal frame going up now. each frame is about 12m span, 6m to the apex. columns are 310UC97 and the rafters are 460UB67. heavy gear

materials on site for today: 6 portal frames complete with haunches, 24 x M20 base plate bolts per column so thats 288 bolts total, plus the apex and knee connection bolts probably another 200 bolts. all 8.8 grade galvanised
also got 48 lengths of C20024 purlins stacked up ready to go once the frames are up. each purlin is 8m long. theyll span between the portal frames at 1200 centres
crane is making good lifts. first 2 frames up and plumbed by 10am. the steel guys are quick
temporary bracing cables are on the first 2 frames to stop them falling over before the purlins tie them together. safety first

delivery of roofing at 11am. 32 sheets of trimdek colorbond in surfmist, each one is 9.5m long custom cut to length. plus 16 sheets of wall cladding at 6m. fascia, barge capping, 8 boxes of tek screws, and a heap of rivets
stacking the roofing sheets was a pain cos theyre so long. 9.5 meters is awkward. used the forklift with extended tines and 3 guys guiding
4 frames up by lunch. halfway. crane hasnt missed a beat. operator reckons its a good day for lifts, not too windy

after lunch we had a slight drama, one of the base plate bolt holes didnt line up on frame 5 column A side. about 6mm out. the steel guys drilled it out to the next bolt size up with a mag drill. engineer on the phone approved using M24 bolts in those 2 holes instead of M20. not ideal but structurally fine
all 6 frames up by 3pm. started on the purlins. got about 16 of the 48 purlins on before knock off time
equipment hours today: 50t crane from 6:30am to 3:30pm, 9 hours. forklift intermittent throughout the day maybe 4 hours total. EWP cherry picker for the purlin work from 1pm to 4pm, 3 hours

material usage: all 6 frames erected. roughly 280 of the 288 base plate bolts used (8 spares). 16 of 48 purlins installed. all temporary bracing cables deployed. no material rejects or waste worth noting
the dog man Barry did a great job today. every lift was smooth, good communication with the crane operator on the two way. no near misses or safety issues
steel crew back tomorrow to finish purlins and start on the girts. roofing crew starts thursday if the purlins are done. should be tight but doable
